summary: The optimal therapy involves local application due to the confined site; thus, using bacitracin as a topically administered antibiotic addresses the infection effectively while minimizing potential complications associated with broader-spectrum treatments such as orally-administered vancomycin. Agent2 reasoned that since the MRSA colonization affects only one specific area—the nose—a targeted solution via topical medication provides both effectiveness against bacteria and reduces unnecessary exposure or risk from other types of medications. Specifically recommending bacitracin highlights how this drug fits well within these criteria by being effective locally yet having minimal impact elsewhere throughout the body.
